name: profitable polymarket boundary puncture

phase_0.boundary_condition: The smart contract perimeter that encloses the escrowed liquidity and enforces the oracle resolution.

VINIT.justification: The state before the prediction market contract is deployed and no liquidity exists.

TANCH.justification: The hermetic boundary that contains the liquidity and enforces the settlement rules.

AFWD.justification: The directed forward morphism of funds entering the market to mint shares.

AREV.justification: The reverse descent of capital when the opposing outcome is nullified.

CLINK.justification: The sequential composition of order execution and oracle settlement.

IMSCRIB.justification: The self-referential pricing model that recognizes its own state and rules.

FSPLIT.justification: The split of the unified escrow into mutually exclusive Yes and No token pools.

FFUSE.justification: The fusion that collapses the token pools back into the base asset, recovering the exact original escrowed value.

EVALT.justification: The affirmative branch where the trader prediction aligns with the oracle data.

EVALF.justification: The negative branch where the opposing outcome is voided by the oracle.

ENGAGR.justification: The paradice where the liquidity provider holds both Yes and No shares simultaneously to maintain the order book.

IFIX.justification: The immutable transaction that permanently records the final payout and boundary puncture.

phase_3.void_description: The uninitialized state before any liquidity is provided or shares are minted.
phase_3.true_description: The affirmative state where the trader position is validated and profit is extracted.
phase_3.false_description: The negative state where the opposing position is voided and capital is lost.
phase_3.both_description: The paradice state where the market maker inventory holds both Yes and No shares, maintaining the boundary without resolving the outcome.

steps.domain_action: Initialize the void state before the prediction market contract is deployed.
Establish the hermetic seal of the smart contract boundary that will contain the liquidity.
The automated market maker recognizes its own pricing rules and self-referential state.
Direct the injection of capital forward into the market to mint initial shares.
Bifurcate the unified escrow into mutually exclusive Yes and No token pools.
Enter the paradice state where the market maker holds both Yes and No shares simultaneously to maintain the order book.
Evaluate the affirmative branch where the trader prediction aligns with the oracle data.
Flow capital forward along the winning branch as the trader extracts profit through the boundary puncture.
Evaluate the negative branch where the opposing outcome is nullified by the oracle.
Reverse and descend the capital on the losing branch as the opposing shares are voided.
Collapse the Yes and No token pools back into the base asset, reconstituting the exact original escrowed liquidity.
Chain the order execution and the oracle resolution into a single compositional sequence.
Inscribe the immutable blockchain transaction that permanently records the final payout and boundary puncture.
Close the boundary as the market reaches its terminal settled state.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: The smart contract bytecode that translates market rules into executable logic.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: The blockchain state transition function that propagates order book updates.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The distributed ledger key-value store for account balances and share quantities.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The block proposer that determines the exact sequencing of transactions.
phase_5.alfs_store: The oracle canonical data feed that serves as the bootstrap store for resolution.

phase_6.cycle_cost: The gas fees and protocol trading fees consumed per transaction cycle.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: The unresolved market state with open positions and active liquidity.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: The resolved market state with finalized payouts and closed positions.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The total liquidity is conserved across the split and fuse operations, with entropy generated only by the external gas and fee dissipation.

    -- reconnect at FFUSE [10]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑱)  -- [10] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Collapse the Yes and No token pools back into the base asset, reconstituting the exact original escrowed liquidity.)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑙 𐑭)  -- [11] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Chain the order execution and the oracle resolution into a single composition...)
